confidenceReason: The latest contract-test sensor run and handler matrix cover every specified clause.
evidenceSummary: Where the contract speaks, handlers agree; one deprecated response field still ships that the contract no longer documents.

basis.statement: The sensor run report was read alongside the clause list; coverage is one test per clause.

findings.condition: The contract-test suite exercises each specified clause against the running service and passed in full on the most recent run.

criteria.criterion: Every specified contract clause matches shipped handler behavior in the latest contract-test run.

effect.statement: Judgments made against the contract hold for the shipped service, supporting the target consistency rating.

evidence.statement: The latest run reports all clause-mapped cases passing; the suite's clause coverage table shows no unmapped clause.

findings.statement: Shipped behavior matches every specified contract clause in the latest sensor run.

basis.statement: Response samples and the contract revision history were compared.

findings.condition: Mutation responses include balance_after, removed from the contract in the v1.4 revision; two integrators are known to still read it.

criteria.criterion: Every specified contract clause matches shipped handler behavior in the latest contract-test run.
criteria.rationale: The field contradicts no clause, but undocumented shipped behavior is drift in the making.

effect.statement: Silent behavior outside the contract invites accidental breakage when the field is eventually removed.

evidence.statement: Response samples carry balance_after; the contract's changelog shows its documentation removed in v1.4 without a removal plan for the field itself.

findings.statement: The deprecated balance_after field still ships but is no longer documented.

summary: Where the contract speaks, handlers agree; one deprecated response field still ships that the contract no longer documents.
